corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: achieves excellent object detection accuracy by using a deep convnet to classify object proposals r-cnn however has notable drawbacks 1 training is a multi-stage pipeline r-cnn first finetunes a convnet on object proposals using log loss then it fits svms to convnet features these svms act as object detectors replacing the softmax classifier learnt by fine-tuning in the third training stage bounding-box regressors are learned 2 training is expensive in space and time for svm and bounding-box regressor training features

context_window.previous: convnet-based object detectors we propose a single-stage training algorithm that jointly learns to classify object proposals and refine their spatial locations the resulting method can train a very deep detection network vgg16 9 faster than r-cnn and 3 faster than sppnet at runtime the detection network processes images in 0 3s excluding object proposal time while achieving top accuracy on pascal voc 2012 with a map of 66 vs 62 for r-cnn r-cnn and sppnet the region-based convolutional network r-cnn
context_window.current: achieves excellent object detection accuracy by using a deep convnet to classify object proposals r-cnn however has notable drawbacks 1 training is a multi-stage pipeline r-cnn first finetunes a convnet on object proposals using log loss then it fits svms to convnet features these svms act as object detectors replacing the softmax classifier learnt by fine-tuning in the third training stage bounding-box regressors are learned 2 training is expensive in space and time for svm and bounding-box regressor training features
context_window.next: are extracted from each object proposal in each image and written to disk with very deep networks such as vgg16 this process takes 2 5 gpu-days for the 5k images of the voc07 trainval set these features require hundreds of gigabytes of storage 3 object detection is slow at test-time features are extracted from each object proposal in each test image detection with vgg16 takes 47s image on a gpu r-cnn is slow because it performs a convnet forward pass

R-CNN achieves a mAP of 66.0%.

SegDeepM is trained on VOC12 trainval plus segmentation annotations; it is designed to boost R-CNN accuracy by using a Markov random field to reason over R-CNN detections and segmentations from the O 2 P [1] semantic-segmentation method.
Following best practices, we performed these experiments on the PASCAL VOC07 dataset.
We applied Fast R-CNN (with VGG16) to the MS COCO dataset to establish a preliminary baseline.
We trained on the 80k image training set for 240k iterations and evaluated on the "test-dev" set using the evaluation server.
Fig. illustrates the Fast R-CNN architecture.
VOC 2012 test detection average precision (%).
Compared to previous work, Fast R-CNN employs several innovations to improve training and testing speed while also increasing detection accuracy.
Fast R-CNN trains the very deep VGG16 network 9× faster than R-CNN, is 213× faster at test-time, and achieves a higher mAP on PASCAL VOC 2012.
Recently, deep ConvNets have significantly improved image classification and object detection accuracy.
We propose a single-stage training algorithm that jointly learns to classify object proposals and refine their spatial locations.
We propose a single-stage training algorithm that jointly learns to classify object proposals and refine their spatial locations.
At runtime, the detection network processes images in 0.3s (excluding object proposal time) while achieving top accuracy on PASCAL VOC 2012 with a mAP of 66% (vs.

3 For the NUS NIN c2000 and BabyLearning methods, there are no associated publications at this time and we could not find exact information on the ConvNet architectures used; they are variants of the Network-in-Network design .

SegDeepM is trained on VOC12 trainval plus segmentation annotations; it is designed to boost R-CNN accuracy by using a Markov random field to reason over R-CNN detections and segmentations from the O 2 P [1] semantic-segmentation method.
We conducted experiments to understand how Fast R-CNN compares to R-CNN and SPPnet, as well as to evaluate design decisions.
We trained on the 80k image training set for 240k iterations and evaluated on the "test-dev" set using the evaluation server.
In addition to reporting state-of-theart detection results, we present detailed experiments that we hope provide new insights.
